signInInformation.title: Changes to Login Process

description.text:
We have merged the login process for EuroLeagueTV and EuroLeague ID – now a single account lets you access both, plus our Fantasy Challenge game.

**If you previously just had a EuroLeagueTV account, please use the forgot password function to activate your new Euroleague ID account.**

If you previously just had a Euroleague ID account, congratulations! You now also have access to a free tier of videos on EuroLeagueTV.\*

**If you are accessing our Fantasy Challenge via our partner's website, please be informed that our partners do not process, collect nor store any user data of participating users as stated in our Privacy Policy**

signInInformation.asteriskText: *EuroLeagueTV is available worldwide excluding Spain, France, Italy and Monaco.

accountDeletionInfo.text:
Before continuing to delete your EuroLeague ID account, please bear in mind the following:

If you play the Euroleague Fantasy Challenge, you may want to delete your team there first, as it will be more difficult to do that if your account no longer exists. You can do that at [euroleaguefantasy.euroleague.net/en/account](http://euroleaguefantasy.euroleague.net/en/account).

club.info: Olympiacos Piraeus has been one of the most successful clubs of the 21st century, with 10 Final Four appearances, six trips to the championship game and a pair of EuroLeague crowns. Olympiacos had back-to-back runner-up EuroLeague finishes in 1994 and 1995, but in 1997 it beat FC Barcelona 73-58 in Rome to secure its first EuroLeague title. After a few quiet years, the Reds finally got back to the EuroLeague Final Four in 2009, but lost in the semifinals to archrival Panathinaikos. In 2010, Olympiacos made the EuroLeague Championship Game in Paris, only for Barcelona to walk away with the crown. The 2012 EuroLeague Championship Game saw Olympiacos rally from a 19-point deficit to edge CSKA Moscow, 61-62, on a famous last-second shot by Georgios Printezis in Istanbul. One year later, Vassilis Spanoulis earned EuroLeague MVP and Final Four MVP honors in London as the Reds overcame CSKA and Real Madrid to defend their title. Olympiacos reached the final in both 2015 and 2017, but lost to Real Madrid and Fenerbahce Istanbul, respectively. Georgios Bartzokas returned to Olympiacos in 2020 and has guided the Greek club to four straight Final Four appearances for the first time ever. After winning its 15th Greek League crown in 2025, Olympiacos will be ready to fight for that elusive fourth EuroLeague title in the 2025-26 season.

heroMedia.title: Strazel’s late heroics lift shorthanded Monaco over Reds
heroMedia.summary: With key absences and major upheaval, Monaco leans on heart, hustle and late‑game poise to secure a dramatic win

content.text:
There was a strange atmosphere at Salle Gaston Mededin when AS Monaco hosted Olympiacos Piraeus in Round 31. Monaco's [Elie Okobo](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/elie-okobo/011212/) and [Nikola Mirotic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/nikola-mirotic/000796/) were unavailable due to injury and [Vassilis Spanoulis](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/as-monaco/mco/spanoulis-vassilis/?season=E2024), who led the Roca Team to the EuroLeague Championship Game last season, had parted ways with the club two days earlier. [David Michineau](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/david-michineau/003836/) and [Yoan Makoundou](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/yoan-makoundou/011935/) also left Monaco this week. And Olympiacos was without star forward [Sasha Vezenkov](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/sasha-vezenkov/003469/), too. 

Despite these circumstances, Monaco fought hard and served up an 80-79 home victory in epic fashion, overcoming all sorts of adversities to grab its 17th victory of the season. 

"The guys showed up. This was an amazing game," interim head coach [Manuchar Markoishvili](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/as-monaco/mco/markoishvili-manuchar) said after the game. "It was also an amazing experience for me. I cannot ask more effort to this team [after] what they showed by themselves. I am not going to talk about tactics, this was an amazing effort, and their effort decided the game. They hustled and gave all they had, being short-handed."

Monaco's great hustle could be seen in the stat sheet, too. The hosts outrebounded Olympiacos 9-1 in the fourth quarter, forced Olympiacos into 15 turnovers and allowed the Reds just 13 assists. On the other hand, Monaco finished the game with 19 assists and 11 turnovers. [Daniel Theis](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/daniel-theis/004132/) led his team's effort with 13 points, 3 offensive rebounds and 3 assists for a PIR of 16 in just 21 minutes. 

"It was a very, very big win, especially with all the circumstances. But we, as players, can only control how hard we play and what we do on the court, and I think we did this tonight. We showed heart and were fighting the whole game, no matter the outcome," Theis said. "Even if we had lost the game on the last shot, we played our heart out. It is a tough situation for us. We are down a lot of players, coaches and like I said, we can only control what we do on the court. We have to play 100% and give it all on the court, win or lose."

As Theis said, the game went down to the final shot. After a back-and-forth battle throughout the fourth quarter, [Alec Peters](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/alec-peters/008829/) tied the game with a layup, 79-79, with 51.7 seconds remaining. Monaco superstar [Mike James](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/mike-james/005985/) left the game with an injury a few seconds after that. [Nemanja Nedovic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/nemanja-nedovic/000848/) and [Matthew Strazel](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/matthew-strazel/008099/) missed shots and [Alpha Diallo](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/alpha-diallo/011226/) fouled Peters, a career 89.1% free throw shooter. He split his 2 attempts, however, and Strazel banked in the go-ahead jumper with 4.0 seconds left. 

Olympiacos called timeout, but Monaco's defense forced [Nikola Milutinov](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/nikola-milutinov/003941/) to take a rare three-point shot, his third attempt of the season. In 283 career games, Milutinov was 2-of-7 from downtown before this shot, which he missed.

💎🇲🇨 Matthew called game.

Against all odds ✍️💯#RocaTeam #EveryGameMatters pic.twitter.com/agy3s5AKY1

— AS Monaco Basket 🇲🇨 (@ASMonaco_Basket) March 12, 2026

"It was unexpected to play with this kind of intensity with the roster we had, but we found a way to push ourselves for 40 minutes and luckily, I had the ball at the end of the game and I am just happy right now," Strazel said after the game. "Whatever happens until the end of the season, that is the face we want to show, and the face we will show." 

James paced the winners with 14 points, 5 rebounds and 5 assists before his injury. [Jaron Blossomgame](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/jaron-blossomgame/011231/) added 13 points. Strazel paired 9 points with 5 assists. Monaco plays three of its next four games on the road, starting with a visit to Anadolu Efes Istanbul on March 19, before finishing the regular season with a three-game home stretch.

content.title: Game News

articleMetadata.title: Strazel’s late heroics lift shorthanded Monaco over Reds
articleMetadata.description: In a turbulent week marked by injuries, roster departures and the exit of Vassilis Spanoulis, AS Monaco delivered a spirited 80–79 victory over Olympiacos in Round 31. Interim coach Manuchar Markoishvili guided a short‑handed team that dominated the fourth‑quarter hustle stats, with Daniel Theis and Matthew Strazel leading a gritty defensive effort. Mike James exited late with injury, but Strazel’s clutch jumper sealed a dramatic win as Monaco prepares for a difficult road stretch.

heroMedia.title: Olympiacos shoots the lights out to celebrate 101st club birthday with road win at Paris
heroMedia.summary: The Reds set a new club record for triples made in a half by going 13-for-19 from deep before the break in the French capital

content.text:
Riding its momentum after a hard-fought home victory in the Greek derby against Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens last week, [Olympiacos Piraeus](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/olympiacos-piraeus/oly/) fired on all cylinders from beyond the arc to crush [Paris Basketball](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/paris-basketball/prs/) 87-104 on Tuesday night.

The Reds celebrated their 101st birthday with a dominant outing in the French capital, demonstrating their prowess on both ends of the floor at adidas arena.

"It's a special day for Olympiacos," head coach Georgios Bartzokas said in the postgame press conference. "Today it's our birthday, 101 years of history, greatness and titles. We're feeling privileged that we're representing Olympiacos at this time and we are happy about our fans who came here from all over Europe to support us."

content.title: Paris Basketball-Olympiacos Piraeus | Round 34 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
Olympiacos hit the ground running, with [Tyler Dorsey](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/tyler-dorsey/009849/) going 3-for-4 and [Alec Peters](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/alec-peters/008829/) a perfect 4-for-4 from deep for a 22-33 lead after 10 minutes. The guests tied the club's all-time record for most three-pointers made in a first quarter by going 8-for-10 from downtown.

"No matter if it's me or someone else, we've got to start games off with that fire," Dorsey told EuroLeague TV after the game. "That leads into the momentum into the first quarter and even to the rest of the game. So, it's important for me starting off games with that fire and being ready from the jump ball to go full speed."

Playing its usual high-pace basketball, Paris bounced back in the second quarter, outscoring Olympiacos 33-30 and preventing the visitors from pulling away despite the Reds setting yet another club record. In fact, Olympiacos ended the first half with 13 made triples – its most in a single half since EuroLeague data has been collected.

"Paris is an amazing team offensively with the pace that they play," Bartzokas told EuroLeague TV. "Imagine that we had 13 three-pointers made in the first half and we couldn't lead by a huge difference because they have this amazing potential and talent to score immediately in the first 5-6 seconds."

It took some tactical adjustments at halftime for Olympiacos to flip the switch defensively after the break, holding Paris to 16 points in each of the last two quarters.

"We talked it out at halftime, made some great adjustments," Peters said. "Their guards were having too much rhythm, they were too comfortable in the first half. So, for us to come out in the second half when they had 55 in the first half and they end up with 87, that's Olympiacos, that's the defense we want to bring."

What a party! 🔴⚪️

Thank you from the bottom of our hearts! 🙏🏽

Once again, you were amazing! 🙌🏽#OlympiacosBC #WeAreOlympiacos #TogetherWeFight #PBBOLY pic.twitter.com/BjsiHqUUYk

— Olympiacos B.C. (@Olympiacos_BC) March 10, 2026

The Reds came up with some key stops on defense and went on a 0-13 surge in the closing stages of the third quarter to take a 71-87 lead into the fourth. Those 87 points scored saw Olympiacos set a second-best club record for most points netted after 30 minutes.

"It's basketball and winning on the road is hard, especially in EuroLeague," Peters added. "If you can pull out a run like that, 13-0, you are going to go into the fourth feeling like you have the game. We kept it there, we controlled it and we were able to finish it."

Dorsey and Peters drilled four triples each, with [Evan Fournier](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/evan-fournier/013380/) netting three of his own. Seven Olympiacos players managed to score from beyond the arc, with the Reds going a sublime 17-for-30 (56.7%) from three-point range.

Having secured its fourth win in five games, Olympiacos is set to travel south to take on AS Monaco on Thursday. And should it manage to combine the first half's offensive display with the second half's defensive outing, the Reds could end their irregular double-round week in style.

content.title: Game News

articleMetadata.title: Olympiacos shoots the lights out to celebrate 101st club birthday with road win at Paris
articleMetadata.description: Riding its momentum after a hard-fought home victory in the Greek derby against Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens last week, Olympiacos Piraeus fired on all cylinders from beyond the arc to crush Paris Basketball 87-104 on Tuesday night.

heroMedia.title: Olympiacos overcomes key absences to stretch winning streak over Panathinaikos
heroMedia.summary: The Reds swept their regular-season series over the Greens despite both Thomas Walkup and Sasha Vezenkov missing out on the Greek derby

linkedIds.text: Olympiacos Piraeus v Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens (2026-03-06T19:15:00.000Z)

content.text:
There are no [Olympiacos Piraeus](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/olympiacos-piraeus/oly/) players who better personify the team's philosophy under Georgios Bartzokas than [Thomas Walkup](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/thomas-walkup/008855/) and [Sasha Vezenkov](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/sasha-vezenkov/003469/). It's something anyone can see, and the numbers prove it.

Just check their playing time, which reflects their importance to the team: Vezenkov averages a team-high 29:01 minutes per game, while Walkup plays 24:59 minutes – the third-highest mark on the team this season.

Yet the Reds entered one of the biggest derbies in the EuroLeague without Walkup or Vezenkov, both sidelined by injury. On Friday night, Olympiacos demonstrated that the foundations of its next-man-up mentality are too sturdy to crack, grabbing an 86-80 victory against [Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/panathinaikos-aktor-athens/pan/).

"Thomas Walkup, for whoever knows Olympiacos's philosophy, Sasha Vezenkov, and of course [Monte Morris](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/monte-morris/014212/) [who also missed out] with his talent, are very important parts of this puzzle," Bartzokas told EuroLeague TV after the game. "But we have a really good roster. We stuck together and played an important game for us because, as everybody knows, against Panathinaikos it's not just one EuroLeague game. It's even more than this."

content.title: Olympiacos Piraeus-Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens | Round 30 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
All 10 players who took the floor managed to get their names on the scoreboard, with eight scoring at least 8 points. Despite Panathinaikos putting up sharp defensive effort in a dogfight from start to finish, the hosts recorded 22 assists spread across nine players.

"All the players played about 20 minutes," Bartzokas added. "We had eight players who scored 8 points or more. It was a team effort. The team spirit was important, especially under these circumstances today. We missed Sasha Vezenkov, we missed Thomas Walkup, Monte Morris, and we had to step up as a team."

And step up they did. [Frank Ntilikina](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/frank-ntilikina/006591/) did a great job containing [Kendrick Nunn](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/kendrick-nunn/012774/), while [Cory Joseph](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/cory-joseph/014316/) delivered on both ends of the floor. The two Olympiacos guards netted 10 points each, with Joseph also adding 4 assists and 2 rebounds in his best performance so far.

"You really have to give credit to the guys who played tonight because they really stepped up," Nikola Milutinov said. "Missing Sasha and missing Thomas is a really big part of this team. The team is used to them being on the court because they make great decisions. But the guys who played tonight – Frank [Ntilikina], Cory [Joseph] – really stepped up and played an amazing game. We're really grateful to have the team we have."

Olympiacos set the tone through its defense from the start, holding Panathinaikos to just 13 and 16 points, respectively, in the first and second quarters before the Greens outscored the hosts 27-18 in the third quarter, turning the game into a thriller.

It’s all love between @Olympiacos_BC and their fans ✊#RivalrySeries pic.twitter.com/wNzxBV4Uyk

— EuroLeague (@EuroLeague) March 6, 2026

Enter [Tyler Dorsey](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/tyler-dorsey/009849/).

Nunn trimmed the deficit to 67-63 from inside, but Dorsey, ice in his veins, drilled one from deep on the other end. [Nigel Hayes-Davis](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/nigel-hayes-davis/008962/) responded with a three-point play, but Dorsey pulled up again from beyond the arc and nailed a second straight triple that made it 73-66 with less than five minutes left on the clock.

"I live for fourth-quarter moments. That's why I'm here every night working on my game for these types of shots and moments," Dorsey said after the game.

Olympiacos forced Panathinaikos into 17 turnovers – six more than the Greens' season average – and capitalized by netting 21 points off them. In Walkup's absence, Ntilikina, Joseph and [Shaquielle McKissic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/shaquielle-mckissic/007853/) pressured the ball, each grabbing 2 steals to secure valuable extra possessions in a tight encounter. Ultimately, Olympiacos's grit on both ends proved to be the answer to its key absences, as the Reds grabbed their 11th consecutive victory over Panathinaikos.

More importantly, Bartzokas's side demonstrated that they know how to make the most of their roster's depth – an essential trait for a team looking to go all the way.

content.title: Game News

linkedId.text: Olympiacos Piraeus v Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens (2026-03-06T19:15:00.000Z)

articleMetadata.title: Olympiacos overcomes key absences to stretch winning streak over Panathinaikos
articleMetadata.description: There are no Olympiacos Piraeus players who better personify the team's philosophy under Georgios Bartzokas than Thomas Walkup and Sasha Vezenkov. Despite being without both players in the Greek derby against Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens, the Reds grabbed an 86-80 victory.

heroMedia.title: Phase 2 general public tickets for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad sell out

content.text:
The extraordinary demand for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens Presented by Etihad shows no signs of slowing down, as all tickets made available during Phase 2 of public sales have officially sold out.

When Phase 2 opened on February 26 at 10:00 CET, fans once again responded in massive numbers. Following Phase 1, which saw more than one million fans connect to the ticketing platform and resulted in a complete sellout, the second allocation was also quickly exhausted, reaffirming the immense appeal of European basketball’s premier event.

The overwhelming global interest highlights the unique atmosphere and significance of the Final Four, with fans from across Europe and beyond eager to secure their place in Athens for the season’s ultimate showdown.

Fans who were unable to purchase tickets during General public tickets sale Phase 2 will still have an additional opportunity on February 26 as VIP tickets will be available starting at 14:00 CET.

Fans who were unable to secure tickets during Phase 1 will still have multiple opportunities to attend European basketball’s premier event. The second public ticket sales phase will open on February 26 at 10:00 CET. The third public ticket sales phase will open on March 17 at 10:00 CET, while the final sales phase will begin on April 20 at 10:00 CEST, coinciding with the conclusion of the Regular Season as teams continue their pursuit of a Final Four berth.

Each remaining phase will feature a limited allocation across multiple price categories, and high demand is expected once again.

All ticketing details for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens Presented by Etihad are available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/).

heroMedia.title: Francisco, Williams-Goss light it up from deep as Zalgiris grabs double-overtime win
heroMedia.summary: The two guards stepped up big time and punished Olympiacos when it mattered most

content.text:
History was not in [Zalgiris Kaunas](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/zalgiris-kaunas/zal/)’s favor heading into the clash against [Olympiacos Piraeus](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/olympiacos-piraeus/oly/). In fact, the Reds had won the previous nine games between the two teams, with the last victory celebrated by the Lithuanian side coming back in 2021.

Yet, that losing run would end on Wednesday night, as Zalgiris grabbed a 99-94 double-overtime victory against the Greek side. An entertaining dogfight at Zalgirio Arena provided fans with a rollercoaster of emotions as the two teams traded blows from start to finish.

At the end, after 50 minutes of a back-and-forth affair, [Nigel Williams-Goss](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/nigel-williams-goss/007982/) and [Sylvain Francisco](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/sylvain-francisco/007975/) stepped up by firing on all cylinders to down Olympiacos.

content.title: Zalgiris Kaunas-Olympiacos Piraeus | Round 29 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
Despite traveling to Kaunas without one of EuroLeague’s top scorers in [Sasha Vezenkov](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/sasha-vezenkov/003469/) (19.3 PPG) and the league's top rebounder in [Nikola Milutinov](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/nikola-milutinov/003941/) (7.4 RPG), the visitors delivered a solid outing, with their defense stepping up to hold Zalgiris to 73 points in regulation.

They say that pressure reveals true strength, with Williams-Goss and Francisco proving that when everything was hanging in the balance. The former netted 11 points, with the latter bagging 10 in the 10 minutes of the double overtime as Zalgiris prevailed.

A bucket from [Alec Peters](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/alec-peters/008829/) was followed by a triple from [Evan Fournier](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/evan-fournier/013380/) for an 82-87 lead early in the second overtime. But the hosts bounced back with an 8-2 run, as Francisco drilled a triple and Williams-Goss added 5 points to his tally.

[Maodo Lo](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/maodo-lo/007032/) hit another three, making it 93-89, but Fournier, who ended the game on a career-high 32 points, trimmed the deficit to a single point by scoring from deep.

Yet, Williams-Goss drilled his fourth triple of the night and split free throws to record a career-high 23 points, leading Zalgiris to victory against his former team.

A victory message from Nigel Williams-Goss himself and a little guest. 🫶🏻 pic.twitter.com/jBAIcXhzLw

— BC Zalgiris Kaunas (@bczalgiris) February 25, 2026

“I’m so proud of my team,” Williams-Goss told EuroLeague TV after the game. “The way we fought and stuck together against, in my opinion, maybe the best team in Europe right now. Just a super, super big-time team victory.”

Francisco also bagged 23 points, with [Moses Wright](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/moses-wright/012878/) dominating the boards and recording a career-high 13 rebounds, which he paired with 14 points.

“To be honest, it was super hard,” Francisco told EuroLeague TV after the game. “It was like a rollercoaster. I think that the team and the staff were really together and trying to find how we were going to win and the way we’re going to punish them and I’m glad we did it. Even if we were down by 5, we came back and got the win.”

This was not just a win for Zalgiris. It was a statement victory against a team that had won seven of their previous eight EuroLeague games heading into Wednesday’s clash. It was also their 10th triumph in 14 duels at Zalgirio Arena, where Tomas Masiulis’s side is a different beast.

What follows might be back-to-back tough clashes away at high-flying Valencia Basket and Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens, but Zalgiris will play five of their last seven games at home, having the opportunity to end the regular season by securing a playoff spot.

And more importantly, with defenses tightening up and games turning into gritty battles down the stretch, in Francisco and Williams-Goss, Zalgiris has two players who can take control when a duel is on the line.

content.title: Game News

articleMetadata.title: Francisco, Williams-Goss light it up from deep as Zalgiris grabs double-overtime win
articleMetadata.description: Zalgiris Kaunas snapped a nine‑game losing streak against Olympiacos Piraeus with a dramatic 99–94 double‑overtime victory at Zalgirio Arena. Despite missing stars Sasha Vezenkov and Nikola Milutinov, Olympiacos held Zalgiris to 73 points in regulation, but Nigel Williams‑Goss and Sylvain Francisco dominated the extra periods, each scoring 23 points to carry the hosts home.

heroMedia.title: Power rankings: After the cups have gone
heroMedia.summary: Our experts offer up a suggestion for every team through 28 rounds

content.text:
Welcome to the latest edition of the EuroLeague Power Rankings. The list is the brainchild of [EuroLeague.net](http://EuroLeague.net)'s team of experts, which is made up of Geoff Gillingham, Javier Gancedo, Frankie Sachs and Igor Petrinovic. The rankings are not a prediction of what the final standings will look like, but rather where all 20 teams stand – in their humble opinions – today, after Round 28.

 

### 1. Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul (20-7)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Besiktas on Sunday to win the Turkish Cup

**The question we are asking:** Is Fenerbahce aware that not team to finish the regular season in first place under the current format has won the championship?

 

### 2. Olympiacos Piraeus (18-9)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Panathinaikos in the Greek Cup final on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Do we need to start worrying about the health of the Reds’ stars?

 

### 3. Real Madrid (17-11)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Baskonia in the Spanish Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** Can *Los Blancos* improve its defense to become a top contender?

### 4. Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Olympiacos to win the Greek Cup on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Will the addition of Nigel Hayes-Davis be enough to put the Greens over the top?

 

### 5. Valencia Basket (18-10)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Real Madrid in the Spanish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** How will Valencia shake its disappointment from not winning the Spanish Cup at home? 

 

### 6. FC Barcelona (17-11)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Barcelona in the Spanish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** With Punter back in action, can Barça improve its consistency and stay in the top-six race?

 

### 7. Zalgiris Kaunas (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Rytas Vilnius in the Lithuanian Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** With six home games remaining and good momentum, is a top-six finish a realistic goal?

### 8. AS Monaco (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Le Mans to win the French Leaders Cup on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** After a turbulent stretch on and off the court, can this trophy galvanize Monaco and stabilize its EuroLeague push?

 

### 9. Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv (16-11)

**What they did last week:** Went 3-0 in the Israeli League

**The question we are asking:** Is Hapoel’s recent slide just a bump in the road or a sign that the team peaked too early?

 

### 10. Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Mega Belgrade to win the Serbian Cup on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Is Jordan Nwora the star who can carry Zvezda to new heights? 

 

### 11. Dubai Basketball (14-14)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Bosna Sarajevo in the ABA League

**The question we are asking:** Now that Dzanan Musa is back and in shape, can Dubai win enough games to make it to the Play-In Showdown?

### 12. Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv (13-15)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Bnei Herzliya to win the Israeli Cup on Thursday

**The question we are asking:** Is there enough time left in the season for Maccabi to climb to 10th place?

 

### 13. EA7 Emporio Armani Milan (14-14)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Tortona to win the Italian Cup on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** Can Milan translate domestic momentum into EuroLeague consistency and revive its play-in hopes?

 

### 14. FC Bayern Munich (12-16)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Bamberg in German Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** What would this team have looked like with a full season under Coach Pesic?

 

### 15. Anadolu Efes Istanbul (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Besiktas in the Turkish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Is Efes destined to become a spoiler for a few teams in the playoff race?

### 16. Virtus Bologna (12-16)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Tortona in the Italian Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** What adjustments can Dusko Ivanovic make to halt Virtus’s slide – especially away from home?

 

### 17. Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Mega Belgrade in the Serbian Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Can Joan Penarroya realign the locker room and restore Partizan’s competitive edge?

 

### 18. Paris Basketball (9-18)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Bourg in the French Leader’s Cup quarterfinals on Friday

**The question we are asking:** What will it take for Paris to recapture its early-season identity and get Nadir Hifi back to his best?

 

### 19. Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Real Madrid in the Spanish Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** After its Spanish Cup success and strong home performances, can Baskonia start winning consistently on the road?

 

### 20. LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne (7-21)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Monaco in the French Cup quarterfinals on Tuesday

**The question we are asking:** With the postseason out of reach, which players are auditioning for their EuroLeague futures?

heroMedia.title: 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad – Ticket sales Phase 2 to start on February 26
heroMedia.summary: Phase 1 general public ticket sale attracted over 1 million fans worldwide

content.text:
Ticket sales for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad will enter their next chapter on February 26 at 10:00 CET, as Phase 2 opens to the general public.

The launch of Phase 2 coincides with the closing of the EuroLeague players’ market, ensuring that fans will have a clear picture of each team’s final roster.

All fans,  except those who have already purchased tickets during previous sales phases or pre-sales , will be eligible to access the February 26 general public sale starting at 10:00 CET. During this second sales window,  all fans will also have the opportunity to purchase VIP tickets starting at 14:00 CET .

Ticketing information is available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/). Fans must log in or create a EuroLeague ID account to complete their purchase. Once logged in, all available categories will be displayed. Due to expected high demand and to ensure fairness, each fan may purchase a maximum of two tickets.

For the first time, Final Four tickets are being released in multiple sales phases. This approach is designed to expand access for the EuroLeague’s growing global fan base, offering multiple opportunities to secure tickets,  whether fans plan to attend regardless of the qualified teams or prefer to wait until the competition reaches its decisive stages. All phases follow a fair access policy, meaning that fans who purchase tickets in one phase will not be eligible to participate in subsequent phases. This measure is designed to ensure broader access to the event.

Phase 1, held on February 11, experienced massive demand, as over a million fans connected to sold out all available tickets in less than five minutes, once again demonstrating the enormous appeal of the EuroLeague’s premier event.

The 2026 EuroLeague Final Four semifinals will be played on Friday, May 22, at 18:00 and 21:00 local time (17:00 and 20:00 CEST). On Sunday, May 24, the adidas NextGen EuroLeague Championship Game will tip off at 18:00 local time (17:00 CEST), while the season will culminate with the Championship Game at 21:00 local time (20:00 CEST), when a new EuroLeague champion will be crowned.

A third ticket sales window will open on Tuesday, March 17, at 10:00 CET, offering another opportunity for fans to secure their seats. The final sales phase will launch on Monday, April 20, at 10:00 CEST, positioned between the conclusion of the Regular Season and the start of the Play-In Showdown, with 10 teams still in contention for a place in the Final Four.

All ticketing details for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad are available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/).

articleMetadata.title: 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad – Ticket sales Phase 2 to start on February 26
articleMetadata.description: Ticket sales for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad will enter their next chapter on February 26 at 10:00 CET, as Phase 2 opens to the general public.

heroMedia.title: Panathinaikos beats Olympiacos, defends Greek Cup title

content.text:
Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens has defended its Greek Cup title by downing Olympiacos Piraeus 79-68 on Saturday night in Heraklion. It is 22nd Cup crown for the Greens, who ran away to a double-digit margin in the second quarter, and never looked back.

Nigel Hayes-Davis earned the MVP award after leading Panathinaikos with 16 points. Nikos Rogkavopoulos scored 14 in 18 minutes on the floor, while Jerian Grant and Konstantinos Mitoglou added 11 apiece.

Hayes-Davis scored 6 points early in the second quarter. Mitoglou’s triple and a layup, followed by Kendrick Nunn’s three-point play capped a 16-2 run, turning a 17-17 tie into a 33-19 lead. The margin was as big as 17 points in the second quarter, and 19 points late in the third quarter, as the Reds cruised to a win over their arch-rival.

Sasha Vezenkov had 16 points for Olympiacos, while Tyrique Jones and Evan Fournier scored 12 apiece. Thomas Walkup had 11 points and 9 assists.

articleMetadata.title: Panathinaikos beats Olympiacos, defends Greek Cup title

heroMedia.title: High-flyers: EuroLeague experts pick their favorite dunkers
heroMedia.summary: A panel of EuroLeague voices spotlights the players turning gravity into a guideline

content.text:
The EuroLeague has always been a showcase for skill, execution and strategy, but nothing ignites arenas or social feeds quite like a thunderous dunk. This season, the league’s most explosive high‑flyers are turning routine possessions into must‑see moments, blending power, flair and athleticism that transcend box scores. 

From [Tyrique Jones](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/tyrique-jones/011981/)’s arena‑shaking slams and [Kai Jones](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/kai-jones/014102/)’s sky‑walking artistry to [Kenneth Faried](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/kenneth-faried/011284/)’s ageless rim‑rocking energy and [Talen Horton-Tucker](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/talen-horton-tucker/014124/)'s bulldozing slams, the EuroLeague’s best dunkers aren’t just finishing plays – they’re redefining how excitement looks above the rim. As a panel of EuroLeague experts weigh in on their favorite high‑fliers, one thing is clear: the dunk is alive, elevated, and louder than ever.

### Tyrique Jones

*Geoff Gillingham*

Is there anyone in the EuroLeague who makes a crowd erupt like Tyrique Jones? Whether in Partizan’s black and white or Olympiacos red, his rim-rattling dunks – especially at home – send shockwaves through the arena. And it’s not just for show: his presence in the paint forces defenses to collapse, freeing up shooters on the perimeter. Just a few games into his Olympiacos stint, he’s already delivered must-see moments at SEF, while Partizan fans still remember the backboard-bending slams in Belgrade. Whether you’re a new fan or a seasoned diehard, when Tyrique takes off, you can’t help but rise with him. That’s why he’s the league’s most electrifying dunker.

content.title: Tyrique Jones dunks in 2025-26
content.description: The best dunks of Tyrique Jones so far in the 2025-26 EuroLeague season.

content.text:
### Kai Jones

*Frankie Sachs*

There is no more prolific dunker in the league than Kai Jones, who has 42 dunks among his 45 baskets this season for Anadolu Efes Istanbul. And Jones is no one-trick pony; his dunks include an assortment of put-back jams, alley-oop flushes, power finishes in traffic and showstoppers in the break. Every time Jones touches the ball on offense, he is a threat to stuff it in the hole. The native of the Bahamas competed in the long jump before taking up basketball at age and the athleticism he developed on the track not only serves him well, but is a blessing to every fan who gets a chance to see Jones fly. His dunking abilities are a rare treat and something to be cherished by EuroLeague fans.

content.title: Kai Jones's dunks in 2025-26
content.description: The best dunks of Kai Jones so far in the 2025-26 EuroLeague season.

content.text:
### Talen Horton-Tucker

*Igor Petrinovic*

While there are certainly players with more bounce than Talen Horton-Tucker, he’s currently my favorite dunker in the EuroLeague. With THT, it always feels like an explosive play is about to happen at any moment, and he usually creates that excitement all on his own, often out of nowhere. It’s his style that makes it so special to me. There’s something uniquely thrilling about the way he bulldozes through defenses and powers his way to a high-flying slam. Horton-Tucker’s repertoire includes a variety of dunks, and whether it’s a Statue of Liberty finish or a two-handed, head-at-the-rim power slam, THT adds a facial expression that makes you want to watch the replay over and over again.

content.title: Talen Horton-Tucker's dunks in 2025-26
content.description: The best dunks of Talen Horton-Tucker so far in the 2025-26 EuroLeague season.

content.text:
### Kenneth Faried

*Javier Gancedo*

Man, I love Kenneth Faried and his overall energy. His first field goal was a one-handed dunk against Paris Basketball and one week later, he was dunking over Walter Tavares! The 36-year-old Manimal is in a great physical condition and that allows him to play above the rim. A. He has been finishing alley-oop dunks since he arrived in Athens, and his connection with Kostas Sloukas is fun to watch. Faried always makes sure he hangs on the rim longer to get Green Nation going. My favorite Faried dunk this season came at home against Baskonia. He took a corner three-point shot that hit the side of the backboard, but got the offensive rebound to fix his mistake with a huge dunk. How cool is that?

content.title: Kenneth Faried's dunks in 2025-26
content.description: The best dunks of Kenneth Faried so far in the 2025-26 EuroLeague season.

content.title: Olympiacos vs. Panathinaikos preview: The Greek derby

heroMedia.title: Olympiacos vs. Panathinaikos preview: The Greek derby

linkedIds.text: Olympiacos Piraeus v Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens (2026-03-06T19:15:00.000Z)

content.text:
Tonight's Greek derby between [Olympiacos Piraeus](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/olympiacos-piraeus/oly/) and [Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/panathinaikos-aktor-athens/pan/) marks another chapter in one of EuroLeague's most compelling rivalries. What was once a closely contested series – Olympiacos led 8-7 this century after the 2021-22 season in their head-to-head record – has transformed dramatically in recent years. The Reds have won the last 10 consecutive derbies, a streak that includes an 82-87 victory at Telekom Center Athens earlier this season. With such overwhelming recent momentum in their favor, the Reds will be confident heading into Round 30, though the absence of star forward [Sasha Vezenkov](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/sasha-vezenkov/003469/) and defensive pest [Thomas Walkup](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/thomas-walkup/008855/) could provide Panathinaikos with a genuine opportunity to finally break through.

For Panathinaikos, tonight represents a chance to upset the odds and reignite its rivalry with a side that has completely dominated it in recent years. The Greens will take heart from the return of [Mathias Lessort](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/mathias-lessort/003842/), who is available for the first time this season and could provide crucial depth in the paint. Center [Kenneth Faried](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/kenneth-faried/011284/) and forward [Konstantinos Mitoglou](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/konstantinos-mitoglou/007866/) were interviewed ahead of the game. 

Olympiacos, despite missing Vezenkov, still possesses the championship pedigree and star power to extend its remarkable derby streak. Center [Nikola Milutinov](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/nikola-milutinov/003941/) and head coach Georgios Bartzokas were also interviewed as the Reds prepare for tonight's big game. As the rivalry's current dominant force, Olympiacos enters as favorites, but Panathinaikos's all-star roster – which now includes last year's Final Four MVP in [Nigel Hayes-Davis](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/nigel-hayes-davis/008962/) – gives the Greens their best chance in years to finally dethrone the Reds.
